phrases.intent_en: Ask about the best mulled wine
phrases.text_local: Où peut-on trouver le meilleur vin chaud ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo puh-TON troo-VAY leh may-YUHR van SHOH
phrases.cultural_note: Try 'glühwein' (mulled wine) at the market. It's spiced with cinnamon, clove, and sometimes vanilla. The best stands have their own recipes.

responses.english: Try the stands near the cathedral, they're very good.
responses.local: Essayez les stands près de la cathédrale, ils sont très bons.

phrases.text_local: Que sont les biscuits de Noël alsaciens ?
phrases.pronunciation: keh son lay bee-SWEE duh noh-EL al-zah-SYAN
phrases.cultural_note: Try 'bredele' — traditional Alsace Christmas cookies. Every family has their own recipe passed down for generations.

responses.english: They're bredele, small Alsatian Christmas cookies.
responses.phonetic: say tah day-SAHMBR, pahn-DAHN kah-truh suh-MEN
responses.local: Ce sont les bredele, des petits sablés alsaciens de Noël.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what time the market opens and closes
phrases.text_local: À quelles heures est ouvert le marché ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kelz UHR eh oo-VEHR luh mar-SHAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Christmas market has daily hours that may extend on weekends.

responses.english: From 10 AM to 8 PM, later on weekends.
responses.phonetic: duh dee-ZUR ah vahn-TUR, plew TAR luh week-END
responses.local: De dix heures à vingt heures, plus tard le week-end.

phrases.text_local: Quelles sont les dates d'ouverture du Marché de Noël ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel son lay dates doo-vair-TYOR doo mar-SHAY duh noh-EL
phrases.cultural_note: The market runs from late November to Christmas Eve. The main areas are Place Kléber, Place Broglie, and in front of the cathedral.

responses.phonetic: wee, may ruh-gar-DAY layz oh-REHR ah-fee-SHAY oh-zhoor-DWEE
responses.local: De fin novembre jusqu'au vingt-quatre décembre.
